Cordia concolor is a plant species within the family Boraginaceae, also known as the borage family. It is a small tree or shrub native to Mexico and Central America, known for its medicinal properties and traditional uses. The health benefits of Cordia concolor include its potential to lower blood pressure and reduce inflammation, as well as its use in treating respiratory issues and fever. The bioactive constituents of Cordia concolor include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, such as cordiachromones and cordioside. The most common herbal preparations made with Cordia concolor are teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support cardiovascular health and relieve respiratory symptoms.
